title: elaphoglossum peltatum f. standleyi
intro: Elaphoglossum peltatum f. standleyi is a fern species native to Central America and Mexico, characterized by its unique, peltate leaves and distinctive fronds. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Elaphoglossum peltatum f. standleyi include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in teas, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
